lore.founded: the Rend mirror of the Concord's Greenguard — sworn by the same forests but opposite side of the treaty
lore.home: the Shadow-Halls — longhouses at the Rendmark edge of the Deepwood
lore.oath: The grove forgives the Concord; the grove does not forgive me
lore.doctrine: the border is the only land that matters; holding it is the whole song

aesthetic.pitch: dark-wood warden in thorn-stitched black leather, poison-tipped thorn-blades, crow-feather mantle, shadow-haunted gaze
aesthetic.palette_shift: Rend black-and-red cooled with thorn-sap green and venom-drip accents
aesthetic.motif: crossed thorn-daggers inside a crow-silhouette

lore.founded: from the Rend-side branch of this archetype's tradition
lore.home: varies by chapter
lore.oath: the forest answered me back
lore.doctrine: the grove keeps its own law

aesthetic.pitch: nature-bound blade-warden in dappled-green leather with vine-stitched trim, thorn-etched shortsword, bare-rooted dagger
aesthetic.palette_shift: Concord palette greened with thorn-sap and forest-shadow
aesthetic.motif: twin daggers wrapped in thorned vine

lore.founded: from the Concord-side branch of this archetype's tradition
lore.home: varies by chapter
lore.oath: the grove's edge is my edge
lore.doctrine: the thorn is the blade's teacher

aesthetic.pitch: wind-bound warden in storm-grey cloak, arc-charged spear, longbow across back, wind-tousled stance
aesthetic.palette_shift: Concord palette charged with storm-grey and arc-white highlights
aesthetic.motif: forked lightning crossing a drawn bow

lore.founded: from the Concord-side branch of this archetype's tradition
lore.home: varies by chapter
lore.oath: the wind brings the word
lore.doctrine: watch the sky; act with it

aesthetic.pitch: cluttered scrap-workshop inside a tent or wagon, rows of mismatched vials, trap-parts on the bench, bandoliers of thrown-knives hanging from hooks
aesthetic.motif: corked flask crossed with thrown dagger under a scrap-sigil

lore.description: Rend's scavenger-craft tradition — small-folk and clever-hands who make poisons, traps, and scrap-agents from whatever the warbands leave behind. Technically informal, but every raid-clan keeps at least one.
lore.founded: in the Hraun landing-camps, born of necessity and salvage
lore.home: no chapter-houses — Tinker-Clans attach to raid-bands and clan-hearths
lore.doctrine: what the raid breaks, the tinker bottles
lore.patron: the Patchwork — the collective pride of scavenged craft
lore.recruitment: apprenticed young; a Tinker's first kill is a thrown experiment they mixed themselves

aesthetic.pitch: alchemy workshop with labeled bottles, copper distillation apparatus, bound ledgers on a reading desk, apothecary-style shelves
aesthetic.palette: amber glass, copper, cream linen, muted green, ink-black
aesthetic.motif: corked flask crossed with a measuring rod

lore.description: Concord's sanctioned guild of alchemists, herbalists, and tinctern-crafters. Small but highly respected; license required, rigorous apprenticeship.
lore.founded: the Compact of Vials — the first Concord alchemy guild charter
lore.home: the Guild House — a compound of workshops and reading rooms in the capital
lore.doctrine: measure twice; mix once; label always
lore.patron: the Three Measures — a mnemonic about ratios that stood in for a patron
lore.recruitment: apprenticed young; a Compact member must produce an original tincture for certification

aesthetic.pitch: underground crypt with ancestor-pole down the center, bound-fetish shrines along the walls, grave-silk streamers and ember-candle niches
aesthetic.palette: grave-linen cream, bone-white, blood-red, deep-umber, ember-orange candles
aesthetic.motif: stag-skull ringed by ancestor-beads on a standing pole

lore.description: the Hraun's oldest religious tradition — binding ancestors into weapons, shrines, and kin. Every Rend clan-hall hosts a Binder-House chapter. Rough counterpart to Concord's Radiant Church.
lore.founded: predates the Hraun crossing; carried across the sea intact
lore.home: the Hollowhouses — chapter-crypts beneath every major Rend settlement
lore.doctrine: the ancestor-chain is older than any oath; I speak for the whole line
lore.patron: the Chain Itself — the collective voice of bound ancestors
lore.recruitment: the candidate is presented to the crypt; if an ancestor answers, they are accepted

aesthetic.pitch: underground library with dripping red-inked pages, bone-lamp stands, ritual-scarred readers bent over grimoires, sigil-marked pillars
aesthetic.palette: oxblood-red, ink-black, grave-silk cream, ember-orange lamp-glow
aesthetic.motif: open book with a single red drop on the page

lore.description: the Rend's dark-scholar tradition — a blood-inked library-cult where every spell entry costs the scribe a drop. Rough Rend counterpart to the Silver Quill but darker, more individual, more personal.
lore.founded: the Scriptorum of Scars, after the Hraun refused to forget the cost of their crossing
lore.home: the Bleeding Vaults — hidden libraries where scar-ink replaces regular ink
lore.doctrine: every spell is a wound; the wound is the receipt
lore.patron: the Unfinished Book — the legend that the first Hraun sorcerer's journal is still being written
lore.recruitment: submission of a rite-scar sigil; admission is marked with the same on the initiate

aesthetic.pitch: moss-covered standing-stone circle in deep forest, antler-crown headstone, flowering vines and deep-shade canopy, lantern-in-leaves light
aesthetic.palette: moss-green, bark-brown, amber sap, forest-shadow, deep-fern
aesthetic.motif: antler and oak-leaf over a standing stone

lore.description: a druidic wildkeeper tradition older than any Veyr kingdom, absorbed into the Concord through treaty. Holds the forests and the wild places in covenant with the civilized lands.
lore.founded: among the Firland clans and the pre-Concord wildkeepers
lore.home: the Deepwood Chapterhouses — circles of standing-stones in ancient groves
lore.doctrine: civilization and the wild are allies when both honor the pact
lore.patron: the Many-Horned — a stag-spirit who speaks through antlers left on standing stones
lore.recruitment: apprenticeship to a circle-elder; a season spent alone in the Deepwood

aesthetic.pitch: blood-stained ritual-ring inside a longhouse, weapon-racks along the walls, scarred initiates pacing, ember-fire in the center
aesthetic.palette: blood-red, black-iron, raw-meat pink, ember-orange, bone-white scar-marks
aesthetic.motif: open-palm bleeding into a weapon-ring

lore.description: Rend's rage-discipline tradition — not berserker-chaos, but taught channeled fury. Warrior-priests who bleed for every rite. Parallel to the Forge-Chapel but where Concord consecrates weapons, the Fury-Houses consecrate the wielder.
lore.founded: a clan-tradition of ritual-warriors who survived the crossing famines by rage alone
lore.home: the Fury-Houses — clan-camps where ritual circle and combat ring share a floor
lore.doctrine: I strike; I bleed; I bless what remains
lore.patron: the First Wound — the pre-crossing saint whose scar is said to have taught every fury rite
lore.recruitment: the candidate stands unarmed in the ring until they break open of their own will

aesthetic.pitch: long-hall interior with trophy-weapons on the walls, central fire-pit, rough wooden benches, banner-tatters hanging from the rafters, scarred warriors gathered
aesthetic.palette: hearth-black, iron-grey, banner-red, bone-white trophies, smoke-tan
aesthetic.motif: crossed axes under a fire-pit silhouette

lore.description: Rend's raider-clan martial tradition — the muscle of the Hraun host. Every clan runs its own training-ring, but the collective tradition is called the Hearth-Broken — those who lost their original hearths in the crossing and forged new ones by raid.
lore.founded: in the first raid-camps after the Hraun landing
lore.home: the Iron Hearths — shared clan-halls where raid-captains muster crews
lore.doctrine: the raid is the economy; the banner is the crew
lore.patron: no figure — the clan-hearth itself is the patron

aesthetic.pitch: training-ground courtyard, racks of consecrated longswords, heraldic shields lining the walls, knights in plate drilling under the dawn
aesthetic.palette: polished silver, royal blue, heraldic gold, clean leather, dawn-cream
aesthetic.motif: upraised longsword crossed with a kite shield under a rising-sun crown

lore.description: Concord's formal chivalric order — elite knights who swear oaths at the Radiant Church but train at the Knight-Order. Produces paladins (combined with the church) and pure knights/blademasters (alone).
lore.founded: the Third Concord — when the Knight-Order was chartered as distinct from the militia
lore.home: the Keep of the Dawn-Sword, Concord's capital
lore.doctrine: the blade serves the oath; the oath serves the realm
lore.patron: the Dawn-Sword — a blade said to have been the first Concord oath-weapon
lore.recruitment: earn a squire's spurs in service to a sworn knight, then take the Dawn-Oath

aesthetic.pitch: dim unmarked hall-room, shadowed figures around a map-table, lockpicks and thrown-knives laid out, single candle-lantern on a side-table
aesthetic.palette: charcoal, dusk-indigo, black leather, candle-amber, steel
aesthetic.motif: single dagger crossed with a crescent moon

lore.description: the Concord's sanctioned shadow-corps — infiltrators, cutters, scouts, poisoners (on a short leash), and lockbreakers. Operates by charter, pays Concord tithe, deniable when required.
lore.founded: the Shadow Census — when the Concord catalogued and formalized its stealth-corps
lore.home: the Quick-Halls — unmarked safehouses in every major Concord city
lore.doctrine: the Concord needs what it cannot acknowledge
lore.patron: the Quiet — an unspoken agreement rather than a figure
lore.recruitment: recognition by a senior, a trial-run, a binding-oath of silence

aesthetic.pitch: open-air muster ground at dawn, rows of pike-rack stands, shield-wall drill formation, stone colonnade background
aesthetic.palette: iron-grey, dust-tan, Concord-blue, wheat-cream, muted gold
aesthetic.motif: crossed pike and round-shield under a single chevron

lore.description: the Concord's formal drill-tradition — infantry, pike, shield-wall, and disciplined unarmed combat. Secular, military-civic, every Concord settlement runs a Phalanx muster ground.
lore.founded: the Second Concord militia reforms
lore.home: the March Muster Grounds — distributed across every major Concord city
lore.doctrine: the line is the law; the line is the life
lore.patron: the Standing Stones — the practice-pikes every muster ground raises at dawn
lore.recruitment: mandatory civic training; advanced ranks by merit and oath

aesthetic.pitch: soaring gothic cathedral interior, stained-glass dawn-sigil, rayed golden altar, white-and-blue vestments and radiant halos
aesthetic.palette: burnished gold, dawn-cream, sky-blue, bone-white marble
aesthetic.motif: rayed sun over an open book, framed by two standing columns

lore.description: Concord's principal religious institution, sworn to the Radiant aspect — a veiled dawn-spirit whose liturgy predates the Concord itself.
lore.founded: long before the Concord; absorbed into its civic structure by the Second Accord
lore.home: the Twelve Halls — cathedral-fortresses with attached scriptoria
lore.doctrine: light is a duty, carried by those willing to stand between it and the dark
lore.recruitment: postulants complete a year of dawn-vigils before admission

aesthetic.pitch: underground vaulted hall with candle-lit pact-sigils on the walls, black-robed figures with exposed pact-medallions, twin daggers crossed on a low altar
aesthetic.palette: void-black, deep purple, pact-sigil silver, candle-ember amber
aesthetic.motif: seven-pointed star with one broken point

lore.description: Rend's pact-bound assassin-mage tradition — where Concord's Night-Quick operates under sanction, the Shadow-Conclave operates under patrons. Each initiate takes a different pact; the Conclave itself serves no unified master.
lore.founded: the Second Pact-War, when shadow-duellists first swore to bear their patrons' sigils openly
lore.home: no fixed hall — Shadow-Conclave chapters move between safehouses in Rend cities
lore.doctrine: every bound pact is witnessed; a hidden patron is a lying patron
lore.recruitment: pact-duel — the candidate and sponsor duel until one yields; the patron's sigil appears on the victor

institutions.name: The Guild of the Silver Quill

aesthetic.pitch: vast library hall with floating rune-discs, silver-scripted manuscripts on reading-desks, tall candle-wards casting arcane blue light
aesthetic.motif: silver quill crossed over an open spellbook

lore.description: a secular scholastic order of arcane researchers, librarians, and theorists. Pre-dates the Concord's religious hierarchy; uneasy but cooperative relationship with the Radiant Church.
lore.founded: the First Library, before the Concord's naming
lore.home: the Vault of Scriveners, cut into the cliffs above Sunward march
lore.doctrine: magic without literacy is wildfire; every cantrip must be citable
lore.patron: the Twelve Scribes — twelve unnamed robed statues in the vault antechamber
lore.recruitment: submission of an original treatise; a senior scribe judges and assigns a working-desk

aesthetic.pitch: windswept plain with standing-stone circle, storm-clouds rolling in, a shaman with crow-feather cloak and lightning-wreathed staff, bow across the back
aesthetic.palette: storm-grey, bruise-purple, crow-black, arc-white lightning, plain-umber
aesthetic.motif: forked lightning striking a stag-skull

lore.description: Rend's weather-binder tradition — nomadic shamans who bind storm-ancestors to their service. Counterpart to the Concord's Circle of Roots, but aggressive, mobile, clan-aligned rather than grove-aligned.
lore.founded: from the plains-clan shamans who first bound storm-ancestors
lore.home: no fixed seat — Storm-Pact shamans move with the herds and the weather
lore.doctrine: the wind knows my name; the wind answers me
lore.patron: the Rolling Storm — a nameless weather-spirit that the Pact treats as kin
lore.recruitment: the candidate walks into an active storm; if they return, they are initiated

aesthetic.pitch: warm-lit tavern-hall with hearth-fire, lute hanging from a post, travel-worn maps on the wall, notebooks and ink-wells on the long table
aesthetic.palette: warm hearth-amber, cream linen, wine-red, road-brown leather
aesthetic.motif: open book crossed with a lute-neck before a hearth-flame

lore.description: a loose confederation of bards, traveling minstrels, wayhouse-keepers, and wandering scholars. The Concord's itinerant-intellectual class — neither cathedral nor castle, but something stitched between them.
lore.founded: the First Wayhouse Accord, granting Concord safe-passage to roving minstrels
lore.home: no central seat — a network of inns, taverns, and road-chapterhouses
lore.doctrine: a story told well changes more minds than a sermon
lore.patron: the Road Itself — a figure of personified journey, never named twice the same
lore.recruitment: invitation by an initiated wayhouse-keeper; audition by an original composition

world.design_reference: WoW Classic zone-level structure — 3-8 levels per zone, shared mid-tier, contested endgame

target_time_to_cap.basis: Classic-style grind tuned slightly faster; strict-coop means duo/trio pace is the balance target, not solo

design_principles: every zone is coop-teachable — no solo-only content, even at level 1
quest hubs cluster into 2-4 per zone; each hub offers enough xp to push ~1 level
mob density is tuned so a duo can level on kills alone at ~60% of quest pace
contested zones bleed PvP rules; endgame zones are the only true raid-scale content

biomes.name: Ashen Wasteland
biomes.description:
A scorched volcanic shelf where the wind never settles. Black soot
streaks the roads, ash-storms close visibility to a few paces, and
pit-vents leak heat through the broken ground. Ashroot and scorched
pine are the only things that hold soil. The Rend treats it as good
country; Concord engineers refuse to map it.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, volcanic ash plain, soot-streaked rock, scorched dead pines, distant smoking pit-vents, low orange sky, painterly atmospheric, dim mood, no figures, no characters

biomes.name: Rocky Cliff Coast
biomes.description:
Salt-wind country. Sheer black-rock cliffs drop straight to a
storm-battered sea, and the only flat ground is the wreck-strewn
shingle below the line. Cliff-wyrms nest in the cracks; raiders make
landfall here when nothing else is watching. Both factions claim it,
neither holds it cleanly.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, sheer dark sea cliffs above a storm-battered shingle beach, salt-spray, low grey sky, distant storm front, painterly atmospheric, no figures, no characters

biomes.name: Fjord Coast
biomes.description:
Cold sea cut deep into the land. Black water in narrow channels,
pine-walled islands, mooring stones cut by Rend longshipwrights
generations back. The deep water means raids land where you weren't
looking. Ice floes break free in spring and orcas trail them in.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, narrow cold fjord between dark pine cliffs, longship at anchor in the foreground, dawn mist on black water, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

biomes.name: Forested Highlands
biomes.description:
Cool, windswept upland country: pine and heather, sudden grey fog,
sheep on the long slopes. Concord tax-collectors learn to ride it;
Rend skirmishers learn to vanish in it. The fog rolls in faster than
men can react and the cliff-edges are unkind.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, forested highlands of pine and heather, rolling grey fog over a windswept ridge, distant crags, overcast afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

biomes.name: Marshland
biomes.description:
Slow black water under a permanent mist, sedge islands joined by
half-rotten plank-walks, the air thick enough to taste. People who
live here learn the safe paths and never share them. Rot-fever is
endemic; the gnats are worse.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, mist-bound marshland of sedge and black water, half-submerged plank walkway, dead trees, low pale dawn light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

biomes.name: Mountain Interior
biomes.description:
High stone country: thin air, brutal weather, the bones of older
empires showing through the snowline. Rockfall and exposure kill more
often than monsters do — but monsters are not absent. Both factions
station outposts here; both routinely lose them.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, snow-capped alpine peaks, glacier valley, ancient collapsed stone watchtower in the foreground, blizzard-haze, cold blue light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

biomes.name: River Valley & Farmland
biomes.description:
The Concord's bread country: warm summers, slow rivers, ploughed
strips between hedgerows, stone bridges, sleepy mill-towns. Bandit
roads run between the hubs after dark; the spring flood takes a few
barns most years. The most stable biome on the island, and the one
everyone wants.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, late-medieval Burgundian river valley with hedgerow fields, stone bridge over a slow river, distant church steeple, summer golden hour, painterly atmospheric, no figures

biomes.name: The Ruin Line
biomes.description:
The scar between the factions. A strip of broken country where some
old conflict was settled badly: ash, charnel pits, the bones of a road
that runs nowhere now. Char-thistle pushes through the cracks; the
wind smells wrong on the right night. Whatever happened here hasn't
finished.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, blasted ruin-line of broken stone and ash, leaning shattered watchtower, char-thistle in the cracks, sickly green-grey sky, oppressive mood, painterly atmospheric, no figures
biomes.climate: scar-land — ash, broken earth, charnel pits

biomes.name: Temperate Forest
biomes.description:
Mild, deep-canopy forest country. Oak and hazel, fern under the
leaf-mast, deer trails worn into the floor. Easy to travel by day,
easy to be ambushed in by night — the canopy hides movement in either
direction. Wolves and bears keep their distances unless winter has
been hard.
biomes.prompt: wide establishing landscape, deep oak and hazel forest in summer, dappled gold-hour shafts of light, fern undergrowth, deer trail winding between trunks, painterly atmospheric, no figures

zones.name: Ashen Holt
zones.notes: Starter zone in the ashen march. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Blackleaf Bower
hubs.description: A forward bower-outpost south of the Spire — woven shadow-branch over a banked fire-ring, six lodge-cottages, a smith and a bowyer. Bower-Mother Sela is the Spire's official liaison with the Skrel of the Scrap-Marsh, which means she wears a permanent expression of constructive frustration.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Darkling Elen forest bower with woven shadow-branch canopy, banked fire-ring, modest lodges in the gloom, dim sickly amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

hubs.name: Shadegrove Spire
hubs.description: The Darkling Elen capital — a black basalt spire rising from an ash forest, banded with iron rings cast in the Coming. Speaker-Black Ravael keeps the inner rolls and a private list of grievances against the Sunward branch that runs to four volumes. Outsiders are received in the lower hall, where the ceiling is low and the light is deliberately bad.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, black basalt Darkling Elen spire above an ashen forest, iron-banded rings around the tower, dim amber-red light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The Burnt Arbor
landmarks.description: A pre-Coming Sunward Elen arbour the Darkling Elen burned publicly in the second year of the Coming, and have never rebuilt or removed. A Reach scholar tends the cooled scorch-marks.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, ruined burned-out arbour of pale stone in an ashen forest, scorch marks on broken pillars, sickly amber twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The Blackleaf Spring
landmarks.description: A spring whose water comes out faintly black and tastes faintly of iron. The Darkling Elen consider it healthful; outsiders are advised to drink something else.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, dark forest spring with faintly black water, mossy stones, dim sickly amber-red light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The Crow Pillar
landmarks.description: A black basalt pillar around which a colony of crows nest. The Darkling Elen consider the crows registered citizens for augury purposes. The crows decline to comment.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, single black basalt pillar in an ashen forest, crows on every branch and the pillar's cap, dim amber-red light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Ash-Grave Circle
landmarks.description: A circle of low burial-mounds that the Darkling Elen tend by rota. The mounds are older than the Coming. The names on the stones don't match the rolls.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, circle of low ash-covered burial mounds in a dark forest clearing, leaning weathered stones, sickly amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The Darkling Elen starter at the western edge of the Ashen March. Charcoal-bark trees that breathe out woodsmoke, banked fires kept against the wind in ringed earthworks, the Spire watching everything from its black crag. The Darkling Elen took the Hraun oath in the Coming and have not forgiven the Sunward branch for refusing. New recruits learn that grudge first.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, ashen forest of charcoal-black bark and grey leaf-fall, dim red banked fire-rings under trees, distant black spire on a crag, sickly amber sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures
zones.vibe: Darkling Elen ash-forest — black bark, banked fires, old grudges.

zones.name: The Ashweald
zones.notes: Contested zone in the ruin line. Targets 31-45 band session math.

hubs.name: The Charred Shrine
hubs.description: The shared pre-war shrine at the wood's heart — burned in the Coming, never rededicated, still drawing pilgrims from both faiths who walk past each other without comment. The shrine remembers the prayer that was lost. Locals say the shrine is patient.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ruined pre-war stone shrine in a charred forest clearing, broken altar, banked embers in the courtyard, twilight oppressive amber light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Ember Crossing
hubs.description: A crossroads waypoint inside the Ashweald — single inn, single chapel, traveller's well that is technically neutral ground. The innkeeper is Concord; the chaplain is Pact. They pretend to argue and split the takings.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, charred-forest crossroads with single inn and small chapel beside a traveller's well, banked embers in fire-rings, dim amber afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Pyre Camp
hubs.description: A pilgrim-camp on the Ashweald's northern edge — five tents per side and a shared central fire that pilgrims from both factions sit around without speaking. The fire was the third Speaker's idea. It has been burning fifty years.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, pilgrim shelter camp at the edge of a charred forest, tents in two colour-tones around a central permanent fire, sickly amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Twin-Oak Grave
landmarks.description: Two oaks grown from the same root at the spot where the Twin Saints of the Charred Shrine fell. Pilgrims from both factions tie ribbons to the lower branches.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, two ancient oaks grown from the same root in a charred forest clearing, ribbons of two faction colours tied to the branches, dim amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Ember Pool
landmarks.description: A black-water pool that gives off faint heat year-round. The locals call it ember-water; alchemists from both factions have come to investigate and arrived at incompatible explanations.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, black still-water pool in a charred forest, faint steam rising from the surface, sickly amber twilight, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Leaning Pyre-Stone
landmarks.description: A pre-war pyre-stone that has tilted three degrees per year since the Coming. Calculations vary on what year it will fall. The locals are placing bets.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, leaning ancient pyre-stone in a charred forest clearing, scorched soil at its base, sickly amber-grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Silver Fern Field
landmarks.description: A field where the ferns came back after the Coming as silver — a colour-shift the local botanists from both factions cannot agree on the cause of.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, field of pale silver ferns in a charred wood, dim amber-grey afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

zones.description: The contested ashland that grew back the wrong colour. Charcoal birch and silver fern through fields that were burned in the Coming and have not stopped smouldering since. The Charred Shrine still gets pilgrims from both sides, which is the only thing the Concord and the Pact have ever been able to agree on. The shrine remembers; the shrine is patient.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, burned-then-regrown ashland forest, charcoal-black birch with pale silver leaves, ruined shrine in a clearing, banked embers, low amber light, painterly atmospheric, no figures
zones.vibe: Charred wildwood — burned but growing back, twin-saint history, old prayers.

zones.name: The Barrow Coast
zones.notes: Starter zone in the barrow shore. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Iron-Barrow
hubs.description: The Gravewrought capital — an iron-banded barrow-hold dug into the largest mound on the salt-cliff. The ancestor-rolls go back further than any Concord record. Speaker-Mother Dheka inherited the Hold from her own great-grandmother by formal consultation, which is the Gravewrought way of saying she asked and got an answer.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, iron-banded fortress dug into a great turf-covered barrow above grey sea cliffs, salt-wind, low cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Silent Harbor
hubs.description: A working harbour on the cove south of the Iron-Barrow — Gravewrought-flagged, Skarn-trading, watched by a salt-wind chapel that hosts a service nobody is supposed to attend. Harbour-Master Brun runs the docks with the patience of a man who has been told at least twice he has nothing to fear from death.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, working stone harbour cove under salt-wind cliffs, longships and fishing boats, small wind-cut chapel on the headland, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Salt-Pillars
landmarks.description: Three pillars of crystallised sea-salt formed by ancient tide-action. The Gravewrought treat them as ancestor-markers and consult them before the hard funerals.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, three weathered salt pillars on a windswept clifftop, crystallised salt-rime, leaden cold afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Shipwreck Field
landmarks.description: A stretch of shingle below the cliffs littered with the skeletons of shipwrecks from a particularly bad winter twelve years ago. The locals salvage what they can; the rest remains.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, shingle beach below grey sea cliffs strewn with broken hulls of wrecked ships, salt-spray, leaden grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Wind-Chapel
landmarks.description: A small open-air chapel on the cliff edge where the wind performs a recognisable variant of the funeral rite, all seasons. The Gravewrought consider this evidence of approval.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, small open-air stone chapel on a sea-cliff edge, salt-wind streaming through the standing stones, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Tide-Carved Step
landmarks.description: A natural staircase carved into the cliff by long tidal action. The Gravewrought maintain it for ritual descents to the shingle below. Outsiders are advised to use the path.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, narrow natural staircase carved into a sea cliff by tide action, weathered stone steps, salt-spray, cold afternoon grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The Gravewrought starter on the Pact's southern shore — low turf-covered barrows along a salt-wind cliff, the iron hub-fortress dug into the largest of them. The Gravewrought make a virtue of consulting their dead, and the dead, on this coast, are unusually willing to be consulted. The First Barrow has begun answering questions nobody asked it.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, salt-wind cliff above a grey sea, low turf-covered barrow mounds along the headland, iron-fortress in the largest barrow, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures
zones.vibe: Gravewrought ancestor-shore — wind-cut barrows, salt, the dead listening.

zones.name: The Blackwater Deep
zones.notes: Endgame zone in the ruin line. Targets 31-45 band session math.

hubs.name: Black-Eel Camp
hubs.description: An exile camp on a half-submerged knoll — the Black-Eel clan's surface presence, a small market in dried marsh-fish and stranger goods. Clanhead Sleth does not personally visit; her hands here run a surface war that does not appear in the Concord ledger.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, exile camp on a marsh knoll, scrap-built shelters and plank walkways, drying eel-racks, sickly green-grey twilight, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Deepwater Redoubt
hubs.description: A surface waypoint on the marsh-edge — three watch-platforms, a flag-pole nobody flies, and a single stone bunkhouse for adventurers between dives into the black water. The redoubt-keeper has a list. The list is of people who didn't come back.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, weathered stone bunkhouse on a marsh edge, three timber watch-platforms above black water, leaden cold afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures

hubs.name: The Drowned Abbey
hubs.description: The pre-Concord river-fortress that gave its name to the drowned-abbey raid — half-submerged keep, banner of a house that doesn't exist, and Warlord Kessen claiming the whole thing in the name of a return that nobody expects. He has held the keep eleven years. Nobody presses.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, half-submerged pre-Concord stone keep in deep marsh, ancient banner over the gate, leaden grey water, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Saint's Landing
landmarks.description: A worn stone landing where the Drowned Abbey's last celebrant came ashore the night before the abbey sank. The footprints in the stone are real and unexplained.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weathered stone landing on a black-water marsh, single worn footprint preserved in the stone, sickly green twilight, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Eelsong Pool
landmarks.description: A pool where the Black-Eel clan's sorcerers sing to the eels. The eels respond. The Black-Eels treat this as a trade secret and have killed for it.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, deep black-water pool in a marsh, Black-Eel clan markers on the bank, sickly green-grey twilight, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Warlord's Garden
landmarks.description: A walled garden Warlord Kessen maintains on a higher knoll, stocked with surface flowers from a flag that doesn't fly anywhere any more.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, walled garden on a marsh knoll with neat rows of pre-war flowers, single chair under a leaning trellis, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Sunken Pillar Circle
landmarks.description: Six standing stones half-submerged in a shallow black-water cove. The arrangement is older than any local memory. The stones are more or less in the same places year on year.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, six half-submerged ancient standing stones in a circle in shallow black water, mist on the surface, sickly green-grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The deep marsh in the heart of the ruin-line — black water, no roads, a sunken pre-Concord keep at its centre still flying a banner that hasn't been on any flag-pole in fifty years. Warlord Kessen runs Deepwater out of the keep and says he's holding it for whoever comes back to claim it. The Drowned Abbey under the western reaches has started ringing again.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, vast black-water marsh, half-submerged stone keep at the centre, distant ruined abbey rising from the water, leaden cold sky, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures
zones.vibe: Endgame deep marsh — pre-Concord drowned country, exile warlord, abbey-saint.

zones.name: The Crown of Ruin
zones.notes: Endgame zone in the ruin line. Targets 46-60 band session math.

hubs.name: Crown Bastion
hubs.description: The outer bastion of the Crown — the contested capstone of the ruin-line, currently jointly besieged by Concord and Pact armies who agree on nothing except that this stone matters. The bastion is held by what's left of a garrison that signed a fifth oath to a fourth flag.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ruined fortress crown bastion under joint siege, two distant siege-camps from opposing flags, smoke columns and trench-works, oppressive grey-amber sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: The Last Candle
hubs.description: A neutral hostel at the foot of the Crown — three bunkhouses, a chapel of no faction, a candle that the innkeeper never lets go out and never explains. Combatants come here under truce and leave under summons. The candle has been there longer than the siege.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, neutral stone hostel at the foot of a ruined fortress crown, lone candle in a window, low siege-camps in the distance, oppressive grey-amber twilight, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: The Throne of Ash
hubs.description: The throne-chamber inside the bastion — broken stone, ash drifts, a seat that the Throne-Claimant occupies without comment. The chamber is entered through agreement of both besieging armies. Everyone leaves intact, by long-standing custom.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ruined throne-chamber interior, broken stone seat in drifts of ash, holes in the vaulted ceiling letting in cold light, oppressive mood, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: Concord Siege-Lines
landmarks.description: The Concord besieging army's main camp on the Crown's western slope. Tents in disciplined rows, heavy mangonels, the smell of trench-fires that have not been cold in three years.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, disciplined Concord siege camp on a rocky slope, rows of tents and heavy siege engines, smoke from trench fires, oppressive grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: Pact Siege-Lines
landmarks.description: The Pact besieging army's main camp on the Crown's eastern slope. Yurts and longhouses in rough circles, a row of oath-pillars hammered into the ground, smoke columns directly opposing the Concord's.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, Pact siege camp on a rocky slope, yurts and longhouses in circles, oath-pillars driven into the ground, smoke columns, oppressive grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Breach Corner
landmarks.description: The point in the Crown's curtain wall where both armies have repeatedly broken through, briefly held the breach, and been pushed back. The masonry has been re-laid four times.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, fortress curtain wall corner with repeatedly-rebuilt masonry breach, broken weapons in the rubble, oppressive grey-amber sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Neutral Well
landmarks.description: A well below both siege-camps that both armies use under tacit agreement. The well is patrolled by a single non-affiliated watchman who answers to neither and gets paid by both.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, single stone well-head on a rocky slope between two distant siege-camps, lone watchman's lean-to, oppressive grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The stone the Coming was about, the bastion the war was fought over and lost on both sides. Concord and Pact siege lines wrap the Crown's slopes from opposite directions; the Throne-Claimant inside the bastion holds a contested title nobody's been able to settle. Walk a quarter-circle around the Crown and you change which army shoots at you.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, ruined fortress crown atop a rocky height, two distant siege-camps from opposing flags, smoke columns and trench works, oppressive grey-amber sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures
zones.vibe: Endgame contested capstone — crown bastion, throne-claimant, both sides besieging the same stone.

zones.name: The Dalewatch Marches
zones.vibe: Late-medieval river-valley frontier; civic, practical, not heroic.
zones.description:
The Concord's eastern frontier — a green river-valley of yeoman farms,
kingsroads, and warden patrols, watched from Dalewatch Keep at the heart
of the Marches. Three crises meet here. East at the Ford of Ashmere,
Pact scouts are crossing in numbers nobody can explain. South in the
Blackwash Fens, the drifters who fled the Censure three years ago have
made common cause with something older than the Concord. And along the
cairn-ridges and copper drifts under the keep, the country itself is
starting to misbehave. The Warden Corps rides the line so the plough
stays in the field — but the line is thinning, and you ride it the
morning you take the oath.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, late-medieval Burgundian river valley, stone keep on a low ridge above tilled fields, kingsroad winding past mills, distant marsh on the eastern horizon, summer dusk warm gold-hour light, painterly atmospheric, no figures, no characters
zones.negative_prompt: modern, futuristic, sci-fi, neon, anime, cyberpunk, concept sheet, character portrait, figure, person, watermark, text
zones.notes: Starter zone in the western dales, redesigned 2026-04 to Classic-Elwynn scope: 12 sub-zones, 4 hubs, 10-step main chain, two side chains, plus side quests and filler.

hubs.name: Dalewatch Keep
hubs.description:
Stone curtain wall, packed muster yard, the smell of horse and forge.
Banners of the Warden Corps hang over the gate. The county seat —
trainers, taxmen, and the chapel where you take the Oath. Nothing inside
the walls is hostile yet, though half the experienced riders have
already been pulled east to the Ford.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, late-medieval Burgundian curtain wall and gatehouse, heraldic banners over the gate, packed muster yard, stone forge smoke, dawn warm gold light, painterly atmospheric, no figures, no characters

hubs.name: Ford of Ashmere
hubs.description:
A forward Warden camp at the strategic river crossing, east of
everything safe. Earthworks, pickets, and skirmish scouts trading
rumours over cold tea. Pact pressure is no longer hypothetical here —
patrol parties have started crossing the shallows in numbers nobody at
the Keep can explain. Veterans arrive from Dalewatch faster than
recruits can replace them.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, forward earthwork camp at a wide shallow river ford, picket fences and timber palisade, distant mist on the far bank, dusk overcast cool light, watchfires, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval Burgundian, no figures

hubs.name: Harrier's Rest
hubs.description:
A wooden chapel to a minor road-saint, with a one-pot hostel tucked
behind the bell. Pilgrims and travelling smiths stop here before the
bridge. Brother Fennick keeps the place — translator and letter-writer
when he isn't tending the lamp. Sheltered from the fens by the upland
scrub.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, small wooden roadside chapel with bell tower, modest hostel beside it, upland scrub and dark pines, overcast afternoon, lantern by the door, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval Burgundian, no figures

hubs.name: Kingsroad Waypost
hubs.description:
A prefab patrol cabin on the kingsroad, halfway between the Keep and
Miller's Crossing. Two bored deputies, a stove, a boot rack, and the
courier relay for the eastern run. Roadside bandit work stages from
here, and most of the Marches' couriers pass under its lantern at some
hour of the night.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, small timber patrol cabin beside a paved kingsroad at dusk, single hanging lantern, courier post sign, autumn rain in the distance, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval Burgundian, no figures

hubs.name: Miller's Crossing
hubs.description:
A stone bridge across the Ash, a working grain mill, and ten or so
steadings packed close to the water. Bread and grain, suspicion of
strangers, short coin. Old Brenn's flock grazes a half-day upriver,
and the miller's daughter has been losing sacks to someone the
deputies can't catch.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, low stone arch bridge over a slow river, working timber grain mill with waterwheel, packed thatched steadings, late-afternoon golden light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval Burgundian, no figures

landmarks.name: Old Brenn's Croft
landmarks.description:
A single farmhouse and three weather-worn barns set against the
western ridge. Brenn keeps sheep and the occasional cow; the dogs are
the best part of the local economy. Wolf packs come down from the
ridge at night, and the croft has lost lambs four times this season.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, single weathered timber farmhouse with three small barns, sheep grazing, low ridge in the background, late afternoon overcast light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Reed-Brake
landmarks.description:
A shallow marsh on a slow river bend, threaded with reed-cutter paths
and the occasional half-rotten coracle. Mist comes off the water at
first light. Corlen's old drifter camp was hidden in an abandoned
fisherman's hut here — the burnt timbers and the symbol scratched
into the door are still readable in good weather.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, shallow reed marsh on a slow river bend, mist rising from black water, abandoned thatch fisher hut on stilts, dawn cool light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: Thornroot Grove
landmarks.description:
A ring of old-growth oak around a black standing stone and a cold
spring. The keeper — half druid, half forester, wholly suspicious of
outsiders — tends a small fire under the largest tree. The beasts
here get territorial when something's wrong with the grove, and
lately something is.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, ancient oak grove around a black standing stone and a cold spring, small fire under the central tree, dappled overcast light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: Sidlow Cairn
landmarks.description:
Low turf-covered barrow-mounds on the ridge south-east of the Keep —
old Mannin grave-sites no one cuts hay near. Local lore says the
cairns "don't stay shut", and at least three of the slabs have been
moved recently in ways no badger could account for.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, low turf-covered barrow mounds on a windswept ridge, weathered standing slabs, scattered crows, twilight cold light, oppressive mood, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: Copperstep Mine
landmarks.description:
A copper vein that played out a decade ago, and that the company
sealed when too many men stopped coming back. Drifter cultists have
pried open the upper shafts and dug deeper — not for ore but for
something at the bottom of the longest drift. The lift cage is gone;
you go down on rope.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, abandoned copper mine entrance cut into a hillside, broken winch and rotted timber, scattered ore tailings, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Blackwash Fens
landmarks.description:
The deep marsh beyond the Reed-Brake. Black water, no roads, the
wrong smells. Brine-shade aberrations rise from the channels at dusk
and don't go quietly. This is where the main chain ends, and where
the sealed confessions of three Drifters were found drifting in a
nailed-shut barrel.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, deep mist-bound black-water fen, broken sedge islands, twisted dead trees, sickly green twilight, oppressive atmosphere, painterly, no figures, no characters

landmarks.name: The Drifter's Lair
landmarks.description:
A cave entrance in the fen, edged with hand-cut stone and the remains
of a Drifter ward-circle. Inside: a tight encounter chamber where
Grand Drifter Valenn finished what the Censure couldn't bury. The
Wake's anchor sits at the back, still humming.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, cave entrance in a marsh edged with carved stone and a broken ward-circle, lantern at the threshold, low fog over black water, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures

zones.name: Firland Greenwood
zones.notes: Starter zone in the central highlands. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: The Greenmoot
hubs.description: The Firland capital — a moot-glade beneath six ancestral fir-trunks grown into living pillars. The Speaker is elected by silence: the candidate who can sit longest without being interrupted by the wind takes the chair. The Greenmoot is opening up to outsiders for the first time in a generation, which is making everyone nervous.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Firland moot-glade beneath six massive living fir trunks, low woven seats around a central hearth, green twilight, dappled shafts of light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

hubs.name: Old-Fir Camp
hubs.description: An old-fir camp at the wood's south edge — half a dozen weathered timber lodges round a hearth that hasn't gone out since the Coming, by tradition. Camp-Mother Velar doesn't believe in tradition but doesn't put the fire out either. New arrivals are tested first by whether they ask why.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, weathered timber lodges around a permanent hearth fire in a clearing of old fir, dim green afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

landmarks.name: The Speakers' Silence
landmarks.description: A clearing where the Greenmoot's Speakers go to perform the annual silence. No outsider has ever been admitted; nobody is sure what the silence is for.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, mossy forest clearing with six worn timber seats around a cold central fire-ring, deep green twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

landmarks.name: The Witch-Birch
landmarks.description: A single silver birch in the wood's east, twice the size of the others around it. The Firland do not name it. The bark has been carved on, by no one anyone admits to knowing.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, single immense silver birch tree in a deep fir forest, scarred runic carvings on its trunk, dim green light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

landmarks.name: The Deer-Path Pool
landmarks.description: A pool fed by an underground spring in the forest floor. The deer drink here. The deer don't all come back the same. The tarn-wardens list the count.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, woodland pool fed by a clear spring, deer trail leading to it, mossy stones, soft green-blue light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

landmarks.name: The Old-Fir Remains
landmarks.description: The fallen trunk of a fir that the Firland insist was alive in the second Concord. The trunk is hollow, large enough to shelter a horse, and warm to the touch in winter.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, vast hollow fallen fir trunk on a moss forest floor, large enough to walk into, soft warm light from inside, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

zones.description: The Firland starter in the central highlands — old-growth fir and silver birch under a permanent green twilight, deer trails worn into mast that hasn't been turned in centuries, the mute Firland working their craft in clearings the Mannin are politely never invited to. They signed the Concord oath in the Second; they've kept it without ever quite agreeing to it. The Greenmoot is opening up to outsiders for the first time in a generation.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, old-growth fir and silver birch forest, green twilight, soft moss floor, pale shafts of sunlight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures
zones.vibe: Firland fey-forest — old-growth pine, deep silence, Concord-allied but not Concord.

zones.name: The Frost Spine
zones.notes: Endgame zone in the iron mountains. Targets 46-60 band session math.

hubs.name: The Frost Gate
hubs.description: The pass-fortress at the lower end of the spine — Concord by garrison, Hearthkin by treaty, both by exhaustion. Gatekeeper Uthar is the third generation in his family to hold the post. He is also the last; his children will not be keepers, and he will not say why.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, alpine pass fortress at the lower edge of an iced mountain spine, stone curtain wall, snow on the upper crags, banners of the Concord, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Sundered Peak
hubs.description: A sealed pre-Coming observatory at the highest reachable point on the spine. The Hearthkin say it's still occupied. The Concord seal it set is intact. Something inside has been answering questions in handwriting since last winter.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, sealed pre-Coming stone observatory at the icy summit of an alpine spine, blizzard haze, dim blue light, oppressive cold mood, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Yeti-Warren
hubs.description: A trapper outpost beneath the upper Spine — built into an old rockfall scar, bone-trophies in the rafters, a trapper named Hesska who has lost three companions and all of her superstitions. The yetis above the warren are her livelihood and her closest neighbours.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, alpine trapper outpost dug into a rockfall scar, log-and-stone shelter with bone trophies, snow on every flat surface, cold blue light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Blue Glacier
landmarks.description: A glacier on the lower spine that is unusually blue and unusually still. Hearthkin scholars believe something is frozen in it; expeditions have not returned with answers.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, immense pale blue alpine glacier in a narrow valley, snow-covered ridges, cold blue afternoon light, oppressive cold mood, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Frozen Seal
landmarks.description: A pre-Coming seal-stone half-buried in the snow at the lower spine. The seal is intact. The Concord set it; nobody admits to setting it. Nobody has tested it.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, pre-Coming carved stone seal half-buried in alpine snow, runic markings clear of ice, cold blue light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Storm Stone
landmarks.description: A standing stone the local weather appears to attend to. Lightning strikes it on the Spine's worst storm-nights. The stone has never broken.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, single ancient standing stone on an alpine ridge, lightning-scarred, blizzard haze, cold blue light, oppressive cold mood, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Yeti-Skull Pile
landmarks.description: A pile of yeti skulls left as warning by trappers a generation ago. The yetis don't approach. New skulls have been added recently, by no one anyone identifies.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, pile of weathered massive yeti skulls on an alpine slope, snow on the lower bones, cold blue afternoon light, oppressive mood, painterly atmospheric, no figures

zones.description: The spine of the iron mountains where the Concord lines ended and the Coming reached its highest point. Wyrms in the cliff-cracks, yetis in the hollows, a sealed pre-Coming observatory at the top that the Hearthkin say is occupied. The Frost Gate guards what's left of the way up and lets very few past.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, snow-capped alpine spine, sheer ice ridges, ancient stone observatory at the highest peak, blizzard haze, cold blue cast, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures
zones.vibe: Endgame alpine spine — wyrm country, sealed observatory, the Coming's high mark.

zones.name: Gravewatch Fields
zones.notes: Mid zone in the barrow shore. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Lich-Weep
hubs.description: A failed-rite necropolis at the field's western edge — the rite set itself to looping three years ago and has not stopped. The Watch-Crypt sends investigators on rotation. Most come back. The site is a hub by convention; nobody actually settles here.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, sprawling Gravewrought necropolis on a windswept turf field, leaning carved markers, low banked ritual fires that never go out, oppressive grey-green light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Mourner's Camp
hubs.description: A Gravewrought outpost on the western funerary fields — permanent black-cloth shelters where the active mourning is performed for ancestor-rite candidates. Mourn-Captain Jeska has been at the post fourteen years and has only once asked for transfer. She has not been transferred.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Gravewrought field outpost of black cloth shelters, lines of low burial markers, banked memorial fires, leaden cold afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Watch-Crypt
hubs.description: The Gravewrought regional capital — a working necropolis with a town built across its surface. Speaker-Auntie Mevra keeps both the funerary records and the harvest counts; she finds the difference unimportant. The lower crypts are sealed pending the resolution of the Lich-Weep matter, which has been pending for three years now.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Gravewrought necropolis-town, low barrow halls with thatched buildings on top, working harvest fields beside, salt wind, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: Memorial Lane
landmarks.description: A long road lined on both sides by low memorial stones. Each stone has a name. The Gravewrought walk the road in ritual procession at the funerary moon.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, long stone road lined with low memorial stones on both sides, windswept turf, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Hollow Chapel
landmarks.description: A chapel whose roof was removed by lightning during a particularly bad summer. The Gravewrought have not rebuilt the roof. The services continue regardless.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, ruined open-air stone chapel on windswept fields, no roof, leaning altar, oppressive grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Ancestor Field
landmarks.description: A field reserved for ancestor-rite manifestations. The Gravewrought ask outsiders not to walk through it after dark. Most outsiders comply.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, windswept turf field with low burial markers in irregular rows, distant funerary fires, oppressive twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Salt Well
landmarks.description: A working well dug into the cliff that brings up faintly salt water. The water is used in the funerary rites and in no other context.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weathered stone well-head on a wind-cut cliff edge, ritual buckets and ladles, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The Gravewrought interior — open turf, wind-cut burial lines, working funerary villages where the dead participate in the harvest. The Lich-Weep necropolis was a routine ancestor-rite center until it wasn't, and the rite has been self-correcting for three years now. The Watch-Crypt sends fresh hands to investigate and most of them come back.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, windswept turf fields above sea cliffs, long lines of low burial markers, working funerary village in the middle distance, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, no figures
zones.vibe: Gravewrought heartland — funerary fields, working necropolis, ancestor-rite gone wrong.

zones.name: The Greenwood Deep
zones.notes: Mid zone in the central highlands. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: The Deep Moot
hubs.description: The Firland deep-moot — a more secret cousin of the Greenmoot, hidden a day's walk further into the wood. The Speakers' chamber here is older; the rolls are older; the silence is much, much older. Outsiders are received only by formal invitation and are watched without being addressed for the first day.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ancient Firland moot-hall built into a circle of massive fir trunks, deep green twilight, central hearth, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, eerie mood, no figures

hubs.name: Hunter's Lean-To
hubs.description: A trapper's lean-to at the edge of the deepest wood — a single shelter, a smoke-house, and a notice-board pinned with bounty bonds and missing-person notices in roughly equal measure. The deeper bounties pay in Firland silver, which most outsiders aren't allowed to keep.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, woodland trapper's lean-to under giant fir trees, smoke-rack, weathered notice board, dappled twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Moss Cairn
hubs.description: A Firland outpost beside a cairn that nobody now alive remembers building. The cairn is tended; the cairn is watched; the cairn occasionally moves in the manner of a person who has shifted their weight. Outpost-Mother Hael has stopped commenting on it.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Firland forest outpost beside an ancient mossy stone cairn, weathered timber lodges, dim green light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, eerie mood, no figures

hubs.name: Wyrd Tarn
hubs.description: A black-water tarn at the wood's heart, ringed by older stones than the Greenmoot remembers. The Firland do not drink from it; the deer do, sometimes. The tarn-wardens keep a count of who goes in.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, black still-water tarn ringed by ancient stones in deep forest, mist on the water, eerie green-grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

landmarks.name: The Deer-Grave
landmarks.description: A small cairn over a deer the Firland buried with formal rites. The deer was killed in error; the rites are a form of apology. The cairn is tended.

landmarks.name: The Tarnstone Path
landmarks.description: A series of mossy stones marking a walking-path the Firland use without explaining. The path arrives at a different place each season.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, line of mossy stones leading through deep ancient fir forest, dim filtered green light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

landmarks.name: The Speakers' Grave-Oak
landmarks.description: An immense oak that grew over the burial of three Firland Speakers. The Firland visit it once a generation. Outsiders are asked not to lean on the trunk.

landmarks.name: The Wolfless Glade
landmarks.description: A clearing where wolves do not enter, by some unspoken consensus. The Firland use the glade for night-camp without ever explicitly explaining the privilege.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, peaceful forest glade with mossy fallen log, soft green light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

zones.description: The Firland heart-forest, deeper than the Greenwood and a great deal stranger. The trees here remember things older than the Concord oath; the deer that drink from the Wyrd Tarn don't always come back the same animal. The Firland send their elders here to die; what they don't talk about is who comes back instead.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, deep ancient fir forest, mist between massive trunks, mossy boulders, dappled green light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, eerie mood, no figures
zones.vibe: Firland depths — older than the Concord, quieter than the grave.

zones.name: The Heartland Ride
zones.notes: Mid zone in the western dales. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Bandit-Watch Tower
hubs.description: A Concord-built watchtower seized last year by a bandit war-band and held since by Captain Byrn, who runs it almost like a Warden post. The locals pay tribute to him and tax to the Crossroads, and the difference is small enough that nobody is going to complain on the record.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, square Concord stone watchtower on a low ridge, bandit-banners hung from the parapet, ditch and stockade earthwork around the base, overcast afternoon, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Flood-Mill
hubs.description: A grist-and-fulling mill-village at the south fork of the Ash, half-flooded most springs and entirely flooded occasionally. Miller Hadda's daughter has rebuilt the wheel three times in five years; the bandits respect the mill less than the river does.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, river-mill village with half-submerged lower buildings, working waterwheel, packed timber steadings, late afternoon warm grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Ride Crossroads
hubs.description: Concord civic crossroads — four kingsroads meeting at a wedge of ploughed land, the post-station and tax-house side by side, a stone tower for the courier captains and a horse-yard always full of remounts. Captain-Provost Aelwyn runs the place by paperwork; the bandits in the wood-margins to the north are her current arrears.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Concord crossroads with stone tower post-station, four kingsroads meeting on a wedge of farmland, busy horse-yard, summer late afternoon golden light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval Burgundian, no figures

landmarks.name: The Flood-Meadow
landmarks.description: A low meadow that floods most springs, fertilising the next season's hay. The Heartland's farmers fight over its margins every year and reach the same compromise every year.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, wide meadow on a slow river bend, partly flooded with shining water, distant farmhouses, late afternoon warm light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Gallows-Oak
landmarks.description: A roadside oak the Wardens used as a gallows in the bandit war eight years back. The oak still stands, the rope-marks still showing in the highest branch. Nobody has been hanged from it since.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, ancient lone oak by a country roadside, low afternoon light, marked branch, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Courier-Relay Ruin
landmarks.description: A burnt-out relay station from the early Concord, never rebuilt. The chimney still standing, the foundations still marked by stone. Couriers ride past it without slowing.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, burnt-out stone post-station ruin beside a country road, single chimney still standing, weeds in the foundations, dim afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Cattle-Ford Pillar
landmarks.description: A stone pillar marking a working cattle-ford on the Ash. The carving says 1417 in old script. Nobody locally is sure what calendar the date refers to.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, stone pillar beside a wide shallow river ford, cattle tracks in the mud, late afternoon warm light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The Concord's interior, where the kingsroads from four starter zones meet the long road east. Stone-arch bridges, watermills working a full shift, the courier relays carrying Warden orders to the Ford. Bandit war-bands have moved into the wood-margins since the line thinned, and the Ride Crossroads garrison is rotating tax-detail patrols faster than they can train them.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, late-medieval Burgundian kingsroad through hedgerow farmland, stone-arch bridge, distant watermill, summer late afternoon golden light, painterly atmospheric, no figures
zones.vibe: Concord heartland — kingsroads, mill-towns, long-distance courier traffic.

zones.name: The Iron Strand
zones.notes: Contested zone in the northern cliffs. Targets 31-45 band session math.

hubs.name: The Old Lighthouse
hubs.description: The strand's oldest watchtower, dark since the Coming and overrun in living memory by a Skarn raider crew who have made it home. Shipmaster Vrekk runs it. The Concord doesn't talk about it; the Pact doesn't claim it. Wreckers below the cliff appreciate the dark.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ruined cliff-top lighthouse, leaning tower with broken lamp-room, raider-banners on the parapet, leaden storm light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Strand Redoubt
hubs.description: A cliff-top redoubt the Concord built and the Skarn have twice taken back — currently Concord by garrison, by trade contested. Captain Marl runs the watch and a private export business that the Concord knows about and doesn't comment on.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, square stone redoubt on a sheer cliff-top above a grey sea, gun-port loopholes, Concord banner snapping in salt wind, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Wreckshore
hubs.description: A wrecker-village under the cliffs — wrecks salvaged in season, fish smoked out of season, and a half-honest captain named Veld who keeps the village's only church bell rung on a schedule of his own invention. The Concord is welcome to visit at low tide; the Skarn at high.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, wrecker fishing village on a wreck-strewn shingle beach beneath sheer dark cliffs, smoke from drying-houses, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Salt-Pillar Row
landmarks.description: A row of salt-pillars formed where the spring tides crystallise year on year. Locals carve names into the soft surface; the next year's tide reads the names back into the sea.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, row of weathered salt pillars on a shingle beach beneath sea cliffs, names carved into the surfaces, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: Wreckers' Chapel
landmarks.description: A small clifftop chapel the wreckers below the cliff maintain by tradition. The bell rings only on storm nights. The roof leaks.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, small clifftop stone chapel above a shipwreck-strewn beach, leaning bell-tower, leaden storm light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Cliff Cairn
landmarks.description: A cairn marking where a Skarn raid party fell to a particularly bad storm landing. The Skarn maintain the cairn from their own boats; the Concord don't interfere.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weathered stone cairn on a sheer cliff edge above a grey sea, salt-stained stones, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Kelp Field
landmarks.description: A bed of kelp that is unusually dense at the strand's far south. Local fishers say the kelp answers to the moon. The moon does not answer to the fishers.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, dense kelp bed on a shingle shoreline below sea cliffs, low tide, leaden cold afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

zones.description: The contested cliffs of the northern shore — sheer black rock above wreck-strewn shingle, watched (badly) by the Old Lighthouse, raided (frequently) by Skarn ships running down the long western coast. The Strand is officially Concord and practically nobody's; the wreckers below the cliffs aren't political, they're just hungry.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, sheer dark sea cliffs, wreck-strewn shingle below, ruined lighthouse on the headland, low storm-grey light, painterly atmospheric, no figures
zones.vibe: Northern cliff coast — wreckers, raid-landings, the Old Lighthouse blind.

zones.name: Irongate Pass
zones.notes: Mid zone in the iron mountains. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Collapsed Forge
hubs.description: The cave-in scar of a Hearthkin forge that vented through the road two years ago. Still hot at the rockfall, still venting acrid smoke when the wind shifts. The lower hall below the collapse is sealed and posted — but the seal has been tampered with twice this season.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, scar of a collapsed Hearthkin forge in a mountainside, broken stone gateway venting smoke, abandoned scaffolds, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Irongate Keep
hubs.description: The pass-keep at the lower switchback — Concord garrison, Hearthkin customs hall, a single trade-court that meets in two languages and adjudicates in three. Reeve Marn runs the customs ledger; Captain Vellan runs the gate. They co-operate like long-married professionals: sparingly and with elaborate politeness.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, mountain pass keep at a switchback, stone curtain wall and customs hall, snow on the upper slopes, banners of two factions side by side, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Snowline Camp
hubs.description: An upper-pass camp where the road touches snow even in midsummer — turf-roofed shelters, a forge, and a mountain-rescue post manned by veterans who lost a season to a slide. Captain Threll teaches the new arrivals about exposure first and combat second. Some of them resent it; most of them survive it.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, mountain-pass shelter camp at the snowline, turf-roofed huts and a stone forge, distant ice-crowned peaks, cold blue afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Tollstone Bend
landmarks.description: A switchback corner where a Concord tollstone marks the boundary of the chartered trade-road. The stone is chipped from carriage wheels; the toll-master keeps a fresh chisel.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, mountain road switchback with a carved stone tollstone, valley view below, snow on upper slopes, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Hearthkin Roadside Shrine
landmarks.description: A small under-rock shrine set into the cliff at the road's halfway point. Hearthkin travellers leave a coin and a spoken name. The names accumulate in carving.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, small Hearthkin road shrine carved into a granite cliffside, banked offering coins, lantern, dim warm light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dwarven, no figures

landmarks.name: Wreck of the Seven
landmarks.description: A pile of broken wagon-frames at a known avalanche corner. Seven wagons in one slide. The drivers were buried where they fell; the wagons stayed where they stopped.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, mountain road corner with broken wagon frames in a snow-fall scar, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Ice-Face Cliff
landmarks.description: An overhang where seasonal melt-water freezes into a sheer ice face every winter and thaws into a ten-metre fall every spring. The road below is permanently sand-bagged.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, sheer mountain ice-face cliff above a narrow road, sand-bag berms below, cold blue light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The chartered trade-road between the Concord lowlands and the Hearthkin deeps. Switchbacks under sheer granite, a customs station at every fourth bridge, snow on the upper stretch even in midsummer. The collapsed forge halfway up still vents heat through the rockfall and nobody has been back inside since the cave-in two years ago.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, switchback mountain pass, sheer granite cliffs, narrow paved road with stone bridges, snow on upper switchbacks, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures
zones.vibe: Concord-Hearthkin trade pass — switchback road, snow-line, customs men.

zones.name: Market Crossing
zones.notes: Mid zone in the southern shore. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: Crossing Freeport
hubs.description: A fortified river-port and free-trade port — six currencies, three flags flying with grudging permission, a customs hall that everyone bribes and nobody outsmarts. Provost Wynna runs the place on the principle that everyone is lying and that's fine if they're consistent.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, late-medieval fortified river freeport, stone quays and warehouses, busy river barges, banners of multiple factions, golden afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Old River Watch
hubs.description: Pre-Concord river-fort upstream of the Freeport, formally garrisoned and informally run by a smuggling ring that pays everyone except the Concord. The garrison-on-paper exists. The garrison in fact has sailed.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, pre-Concord stone river fortress on a bend of a slow river, half-overgrown walls, smugglers' boats moored below, twilight grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Silver Caravanserai
hubs.description: The largest of the trade-houses outside the Freeport wall — a Hraun-funded compound of stables, warehouses and a tea-house that is the polite front for at least three intelligence services. Caravan-Master Thessa considers herself neutral; the Freeport considers her useful.

landmarks.name: The River-Island Market
landmarks.description: An island market that runs three days a week on a sand-bar in the river. Six languages, three currencies, no formal taxes. The Freeport tolerates it because the Freeport profits from it.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, busy market on a river sand-bar island, stalls and barges drawn up to the banks, golden afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Smuggler's Cove
landmarks.description: A reed-fringed bend of the river where the smugglers unload after dark. The Freeport's customs hall pretends not to know. The cove's mooring posts are very new.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, reed-fringed river cove with mooring posts and dark barges, low twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: Pre-Concord Pillar
landmarks.description: A standing stone carved with a script even the Sunward Elen scholars cannot quite parse. The stone is on Freeport land by treaty; nobody removes it because no faction will risk it.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weathered standing stone on the bank of a slow river, unreadable carvings, late afternoon warm light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Burnt Row
landmarks.description: A row of warehouses that burned in last spring's fire. The owners cleared the rubble; the foundations are still ashy. Three of the owners did not survive the fire.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, row of burnt-out warehouse foundations on a river quay, blackened stone, low afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: Where the southern shore meets the river road — a fortified freeport of warehouse compounds, river-quays, six competing currencies and one shared language of complaint. Concord-controlled but Hraun-tolerant; the silver caravan houses pay both flags and answer to neither. The old river watch upstream has been overrun by smugglers who pay everyone except the Concord.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, late-medieval river-port freeport, stone quay, warehouse compounds, busy river barges, golden afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, no figures
zones.vibe: Concord trade hub — caravanserai, river-port, civic noise.

zones.name: The Pact Causeway
zones.notes: Mid zone in the pact steppes. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: Causeway Hold
hubs.description: The Causeway capital — a hold built into a mile-long stone causeway along the steppe ridge, a road that is also a fortress and also an archive. Hold-Master Karul keeps three different archives in three different languages and considers the difference between them his life's work.
