hubs.name: The Oath Pillar
hubs.description: A pillar-shrine where the Causeway's oldest standing oath-stone meets a buried reliquary. The reliquary was sealed by the founding Speaker for a reason. Someone has been digging at the seal and leaving — the scribes insist — no tracks at all.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ancient Kharun oath-pillar shrine on a high steppe ridge, half-uncovered buried reliquary stones, wind-driven cloud shadows, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Stone-Reader Camp
hubs.description: A scribal outpost halfway down the Causeway — three stone-reader scribes who are formally not allowed to argue and informally do nothing else. Their job is to transcribe new oath-pillar carvings and forward them to the Hold. The job's hidden purpose is to find out who's adding to the carvings without authority.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Kharun scribal outpost beside a row of carved oath-pillars on a stone causeway, three small yurts, ink stones and weighted scrolls, overcast cool light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.description: A grove of stunted steppe-junipers planted by the founding Speaker, each one carrying a single bound oath. The trees are pruned annually by Hold scribes.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, grove of stunted juniper trees on a windswept causeway side, small tags hanging from each, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.name: The Scribe's Cave
landmarks.description: A cave below the causeway where a scribe kept the original oath-rolls during the winter of the Coming. The rolls are now in the Hold; the cave is preserved.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, small cave entrance below a stone causeway ridge, weathered scribal markings on the walls, lantern-lit interior, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.name: The Standing-Horse Stones
landmarks.description: Three stones carved into rough horse-shape, said by the Kharun to mark a foundational herd-vow. The stones are old enough that the carving has weathered into suggestion.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, three weathered horse-shaped standing stones on a windswept steppe slope, leaden afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.name: The Fallen-Pillar Field
landmarks.description: A field where seven oath-pillars have fallen over the centuries. The Speaker visits the field annually. The fallen pillars are not raised again.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, windswept field with seven fallen carved oath-pillars in a rough line, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

zones.description: A long Kharun stone-causeway through the central pact steppes, lined with oath-pillars older than any reigning house. The Pact-Speaker's scribes still road-walk it monthly, reading the carvings and reporting any new ones to the Hold. The buried Oath-Pillar Vault holds reliquary secrets the Speaker would prefer remained buried, which naturally is exactly why someone is digging it up.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, long stone causeway across windswept highland steppe, lines of carved oath-pillars on either side, distant storm front, cool grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures
zones.vibe: Kharun stone-roads — buried oaths, traveling scribes, formal violence.

zones.name: The Pactmarch
zones.notes: Starter zone in the pact steppes. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Pactstone Hold
hubs.description: The Kharun starter capital — a low-walled stone hold at the meeting of two oath-roads, with the great Pactstone of the Hold's founding still standing in the central court. Hold-Master Tehkur signed his oath here in his fifteenth year and has not violated it since, which is less impressive when you understand he literally cannot.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Kharun stone hold on a steppe ridge, low curtain wall around a central pactstone monument, windswept grasslands, overcast cool light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

hubs.name: Scholar's Camp
hubs.description: A scribal camp on the Hold's eastern slope — three yurts, a flat-stone reading table that holds the wind, and the Hold's archive copies for road-walking review. Scribe Velm has been reading the same oath-pillar inscription for five years, getting different things from it, and writing them all down.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Kharun scribal camp on a windswept steppe slope, three yurts and a stone reading table, ink stones and weighted scroll-cases, overcast afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.name: The Bound Field
landmarks.description: A flat steppe-meadow where Kharun oath-bindings are formally performed. The grass grows differently here in a circle around the central pactstone; nobody is sure why.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, flat steppe meadow with central standing pactstone, ring of slightly different grass colour around it, wide horizon, overcast cool light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.name: The Herd-Stone
landmarks.description: A great stone the Kharun herd-clans rotate around in their annual passage. The stone marks a covenant the Hold remembers in writing; the herd-clans remember it in routine.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, great rounded standing stone on a windswept steppe ridge, herd tracks worn around its base, overcast afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.name: The Yurt-Shrine
landmarks.description: A permanent yurt set up by a single Kharun ascetic who has kept watch on a particular oath-stone for forty years. He no longer takes visitors. He does still take supplies.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, single weathered Kharun yurt on a windswept steppe slope, beside a tall oath-stone, overcast cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe, no figures

landmarks.name: The Broken Pillar
landmarks.description: A Kharun oath-pillar broken in half — by what nobody is willing to say out loud. The Hold has not removed the fragments. The fragments have not weathered.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, fallen broken oath-pillar in two pieces on a windswept steppe, carvings still sharp, oppressive overcast sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The Kharun starter on the western Pact steppes — long windswept grasslands punctuated by oath-stones, herd-ways, and the great Pactstone Hold at their meeting. The Kharun are oath-bound to a degree that mystifies outsiders: they consider a broken word a worse crime than murder. Every scholar in the Hold can name the day a vow was first carried east.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, vast windswept highland steppe, ancient standing oath-stones in a circle, distant fortress on a low hill, overcast cool light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval steppe culture, no figures
zones.vibe: Kharun oath-steppe — long horizons, bound stones, blood-formal manners.

zones.name: The Ruin Line — Northern Scar
zones.notes: Contested zone in the ruin line. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: The Burned Abbey
hubs.description: What's left of the Concord's spiritual seat for the northern frontier — burned, sealed, but still drawing pilgrims from both sides who know perfectly well it is no longer holy and pretend otherwise. The abbess is still inside, by report. Officially.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ruined Concord stone abbey blackened by fire, half-collapsed cloister, char-thistle in the courtyard, oppressive grey-green sky, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: No-Man's Ridge
hubs.description: An outpost on the ridge between the Concord and Pact trenches — a single watch-house, a stocked rope-bridge in either direction, and a deal between the two captains that is honoured because neither of them would survive having to break it.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, lone stone watch-house on a windswept ridge between two distant trench-camps, rope-bridge to either side, sickly amber-green sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Scarhold
hubs.description: A way-fort on the northern edge of the scar — Concord by treaty, contested in fact, garrisoned by Wardens and Pact-Speakers in alternating shifts that don't overlap by more than an hour. The hold's well still works; the bell-tower has not rung since the Coming.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, contested stone way-fort at the edge of a blasted ruin-line, two faction banners flying side by side, broken bell-tower, sickly grey-green sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Burnt Orchard
landmarks.description: A pre-war Concord orchard burned in the Coming. The trees came back; the apples are smoky-tasting and grey. Both factions agree not to harvest.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, burnt orchard regrowing with grey-leafed trees on a windswept ridge, sickly grey-green light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Trench Line
landmarks.description: A line of pre-war trenches where the first Concord-Pact engagement of the Coming happened. The trenches are preserved by both factions, by separate agreement.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weathered preserved pre-war trench line on a windswept ridge, broken weapons in the bottom, oppressive grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Crow Field
landmarks.description: A field where the local crow population has settled permanently since the Coming. The crows behave oddly here. They do not flock when disturbed.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, windswept ruin-line field full of crows standing on the ground rather than flying, sickly grey-green light, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Memorial Arch
landmarks.description: A stone arch raised by both factions after the Coming, carrying both their inscriptions. The arch is a rare shared monument; both factions agree it has nothing to do with reconciliation.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weathered stone memorial arch on a blasted ruin-line ridge, dual inscriptions in two scripts, oppressive grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The northern half of the contested ruin-line — a hundred-league strip of burned ground between the Concord lowlands and the Pact steppes. Ploughs don't go in; the wind smells of old smoke and something older. The Burned Abbey at the scar's heart used to be the Concord's spiritual seat for this country, and walking through what's left of it is its own kind of pilgrimage.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, blasted ruin-line of broken stone and ash, leaning shattered tower, char-thistle in the cracks, sickly grey-green sky, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures
zones.vibe: Northern Scar — burned-out frontier, open grave-pits, wind that smells wrong.

zones.name: The Ruin Line — Southern Scar
zones.notes: Contested zone in the ruin line. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: Southscar Camp
hubs.description: A way-station camp on the southern scar's main road — a shared post by treaty, four bunkhouses, one inn, and a single bell that rings only when both faction captains agree, which means almost never. Captain Halvern of the Concord watch and Speaker-Hand Yruk of the Pact watch are not friends. They are professionals.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, contested way-station camp on a blasted scarland road, two-flag bunkhouses around a shared bell-post, leaden grey-amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: The Sunken Tower
hubs.description: A Sunward Elen arcane spire half-submerged in the salt marsh that grew over the southern scar. The tower still argues with itself in three voices, none of which match any record. Outsiders are warned to stand at the marsh-edge and not enter the water. Locals do not require warning.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, half-submerged Sunward Elen arcane spire in a salt marsh, leaning broken pinnacle, sickly blue-green glow at the water level, oppressive twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Field of Two Kings
hubs.description: A field outpost above what locals call the Field of Two Kings — turf earthworks, a single inn, a chapel that answers to neither faction's calendar. The two Kings fell here. Nobody remembers exactly which two.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, turf earthwork outpost above an old battle field, half-buried banners and broken weapons in the grass, lone roadside chapel, oppressive grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Kings' Field
landmarks.description: A flat field where the locals say two kings died. The broken weapons in the grass haven't been removed since the Coming. Pilgrims from both sides walk it.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, windswept flat field on a ruin-line ridge, half-buried banners and broken weapons in the grass, oppressive grey-amber sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Salt-Marsh Chapel
landmarks.description: A small stone chapel half-submerged in the salt marsh that ate the southern scar. The bell still rings on a wind from the right direction. The bell-ringer is, by record, dead.

landmarks.name: The Watch Grave
landmarks.description: A single grave on a knoll above the marsh — Captain Erren of the Concord and Speaker-Hand Vekel of the Pact buried side by side, by joint agreement, in a year that nobody publishes.

landmarks.name: The Standing Blade
landmarks.description: A pre-war Sunward Elen sword driven into a stone outcrop and never removed, despite both factions claiming the right. The blade is rust-free.

zones.description: The southern half of the ruin-line — lower country, wetter, sunken fortifications half-eaten by salt-marsh since the Coming. The Sunken Tower was a Sunward Elen arcane spire before the war ended its function; it still leaks the kind of magic that argues with itself. Every second campsite around the Field of Two Kings has a story about hearing footsteps with no one walking.

zones.vibe: Southern Scar — sunken fortifications, drowned arcana, two-king history.

zones.name: The Scrap Flats
zones.notes: Mid zone in the ashen march. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: The Great Warren
hubs.description: The Skrel mid-capital — a deep-warren capital under the alchemical works, lit by phosphor-fungus and ventilated by stolen Hearthkin shaft-design. Boss-of-Bosses Vrag keeps the rolls and the recipes; she does not let outsiders see either. Pretend not to notice and you'll be fine.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, vast Skrel under-warren capital under alchemical works, phosphor-fungus glow, scaffolded ventilation shafts, sickly green light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Pit-Tinker Works
hubs.description: The pit-works that the tinkers actually live in — a vertical Skrel industrial complex of conveyor-pits, vat-tanks and scaffolded gantries. Site-Boss Glub runs the works and considers explosions a normal cost of doing business; the Hold considers them political when they damage anything important.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, vertical Skrel industrial pit complex, scaffold gantries and conveyor pits, vat-tanks of acid and steam, sickly green smoke columns, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Rust Creek
hubs.description: A waste-water Skrel outpost on the eastern Flats — built around a rust-creek that runs the colour of dried blood from upstream tinker-vats. The locals call it good fishing water and the Hold prefers not to ask why.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Skrel outpost beside a rust-red runoff creek, plank walkways and scrap shelters, sickly amber-green dusk, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Scavenger Mound
hubs.description: A scavenger-camp on the western marsh edge — half a warren, half a workshop, full of Skrel who specialise in extracting valuable Hearthkin scrap from very old wrecks. The scrap, unfortunately, sometimes objects to being extracted.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Skrel scavenger mound camp on a marsh flat, salvaged metal sheds and tarps, vat-tanks of acid, low pale light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Conveyor Skeleton
landmarks.description: A pre-Coming conveyor scaffold the Skrel kept upright because it works. The conveyor is patched with a hundred different alloys and produces an unpredictable noise.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, towering pre-Coming industrial conveyor scaffold patched with mixed metals, smoke columns, sickly green light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Vat Field
landmarks.description: A field of open vat-tanks at varying acid concentrations. The Skrel maintain them by formal rota. The Hearthkin will not approach within a kilometre.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, open field full of square acid vat-tanks, sickly green-amber light, scrap-built walkways between them, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Alloy Pillar
landmarks.description: A pillar cast from two centuries of failed alloy experiments, layered like a tree-ring. Skrel scholars come to read it; they leave with arguments.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, tall layered alloy pillar in a marshy industrial flat, sickly green-amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Rust Field
landmarks.description: A field where every salvaged piece of Concord iron has gone to rust. The Skrel keep it as inventory. The inventory is extensive.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, vast field of rusted salvaged iron pieces in disorderly piles, sickly amber-green dusk, painterly atmospheric, no figures

zones.description: The Skrel industrial heart, north of the Scrap Marsh — full alchemical works, conveyor pits, vat-tanks of questionable acid going through dubious processes for rumoured Hraun contracts. The Great Warren under the works is the Skrel capital in everything but politics. Tinker explosions are routine; tinker successes are political.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, sprawling alchemical scavenger-industrial works on a marshy flat, vat-tanks and conveyors, sickly green smoke columns, dim amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, no figures
zones.vibe: Skrel industrial sprawl — alchemy-works, conveyor pits, acid sumps.

zones.name: The Scrap-Marsh
zones.notes: Starter zone in the ashen march. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: The Junk-Warren
hubs.description: The Skrel starter capital — a labyrinth of alchemical scrap-warrens dug into and through and under a hill of rusted metal. Boss-of-Bosses Skrenn runs it on a principle of constructive bribery. The plank-walks outside the warren are mapped by colour; the warren itself is mapped by smell.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, sprawling Skrel scrap-mound town, warren entrances dug into rusted metal hill, plank walkways, sickly green alchemical smoke, low pale light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Trap-Post
hubs.description: A Skrel outpost on the road in — three trap-fields, a watch-warren, and a single welcoming-house run by a tinker who lost half a hand to a previous welcome. The traps are explicit because the explicit traps protect the implicit ones.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Skrel trap-post on a marsh road, warren entrance, scrap-built watchtower, half-rotten plank walkways, sickly amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Rusted Wagon Field
landmarks.description: A flat sump where Skrel scavengers stack salvaged Hearthkin wagons until somebody decides what to do with them. The stacks have been growing for six years.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, marshy field full of rusted broken Hearthkin wagons stacked in disorderly piles, sickly green-amber light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Acid Pool
landmarks.description: A deep pool of by-product alchemy that the Skrel have weighed for hazard and pronounced acceptable. Outsiders are advised to circle widely. Wildlife near the pool is unusual.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, dark green-yellow acid pool in a marsh, warning sticks driven into the mud at the edges, sickly amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Warren Smoke-Vent
landmarks.description: A vent where the Skrel under-warrens release alchemical smoke. The smoke is colour-coded by chemistry, and the Skrel watch the colour from a kilometre away.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, marsh ground vent leaking sickly yellow-green smoke, scrap-built ring of stones around it, sickly amber-green light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

landmarks.name: The Sunken Skrel Shrine
landmarks.description: A half-submerged shrine to the patron of opportunistic salvage, dedicated by an unnamed Skrel scholar who never claimed credit. Offerings are by tradition broken-but-valuable.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, half-submerged scrap-built shrine in a marsh, broken pieces of salvaged metal hung as offerings, sickly green twilight, painterly atmospheric, no figures

zones.description: The Skrel starter at the southern edge of the Ashen March — a swamp of rusted scrap-piles, half-rotten plank-walks, and alchemical sumps that smell different every hour. The Skrel are scavengers, alchemists, and unparalleled at getting more out of broken things than was ever put in. The Junk-Warren beneath their main town is a minor folk wonder. The traps are not.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, rusted scrap-mire, half-sunken plank walkways between mounds of broken metal, sickly green alchemical mist, low pale light, painterly atmospheric, no figures
zones.vibe: Skrel scavenger-mire — rust, alchemy, traps for the unwary.

zones.name: Shadegrove
zones.notes: Mid zone in the ashen march. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Cinder Post
hubs.description: A volcanic-soil outpost in the lower forest — turf-roofed shelters, a smith working volcanic-glass blades, a patrol-house manned by Reach veterans who lost a feud and are too old to fight a new one. The grove-cult to the south uses the post's roads without asking.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ash-forest outpost on volcanic soil, turf-roofed shelters, smithy with banked red coals, dim amber afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

hubs.name: Dark Glade
hubs.description: A glade-shrine that the Reach officially does not endorse. The Pact-Speakers visit it; the Speakers do not say so out loud. The cultists in the deeper sanctum have begun signing their letters with a glyph the Reach scholars have politely declined to translate.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ash-forest glade with a half-buried shadow-shrine, banked obsidian fire, hanging silver leaves, sickly amber dusk, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures

hubs.name: Shadegrove Reach
hubs.description: The Darkling Elen mid-capital — a black-iron Reach fortress on a high crag above the silver-ash forest, banners of cooled obsidian, an under-gallery the Spire publicly does not visit and privately controls. Reach-Mother Velna keeps the Spire's polite distance by enforcing it personally.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, black-iron Darkling Elen fortress on a sheer crag above silver-ash forest, obsidian banners, sickly amber-red dusk, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The Witch-Cinder Pyre
landmarks.description: A pyre-pit the Reach maintains for ritual cremations of matters they prefer cremated. The pit is never cold and never explained.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, deep stone pyre-pit on volcanic soil, embers always burning, ash drifts, sickly amber-red light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Silver-Bone Cairn
landmarks.description: A cairn of weathered animal bones bleached to silver. The Reach scholars insist they are not human. The Reach scholars have a very specific definition of human.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, mossy stone cairn topped with weathered silver-bleached animal bones, ashen forest around it, dim amber-grey light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The Hidden Glade
landmarks.description: A glade nobody official will admit to using. The fire-ring is fresh; the offerings are not. Reach patrols visit regularly and report finding nothing of note.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, secluded ash-forest glade with fresh fire-ring and broken offerings, sickly amber dusk, oppressive mood, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The Black-Iron Gate
landmarks.description: A free-standing iron gate set in the middle of a clearing with no wall on either side. The gate is locked. The lock is ancient and untouched and somehow not rusted.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, black wrought-iron gate standing free in an ash-forest clearing with no walls, ancient unrusted lock, oppressive amber-red dusk, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures

zones.description: The Darkling Elen mid-zone in the Ashen March — silver-ash trees on a slope of grey volcanic soil, the Reach looming above with its black-iron banners, a cult operating out of the Dark Glade Sanctum that the Reach officially does not employ and unofficially does not discourage. New arrivals learn quickly which questions to leave alone.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, silver-ash forest on volcanic soil, dark fortress reach on a high crag, banked red coals in the foreground, sickly amber light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dark elf, no figures
zones.vibe: Darkling Elen ashland — silver-black trees, hidden cult, the Reach watching.

zones.name: Silverleaf Wood
zones.notes: Mid zone in the central highlands. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: Elder Grove
hubs.description: A pre-Veyr ruin at the wood's northern edge — moss-buried stone, a single standing column, and a way-station built against it by Sunward Elen scholars who say the column is still in use. The Elder Grove ruin proper begins where the moss thickens.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, mossy pre-Veyr ruin pillar at the edge of a silver birch forest, small scholar way-station built against it, dappled light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

hubs.name: Moth-Hollow
hubs.description: A clearing-village in the wood's east — moth-flight every twilight, a small market of silk and dye, a quiet alchemist's house run by a Sunward Elen who refuses on principle to take a Mannin honorific. The hollow's lower passages are off-limits and the reasons are off the record.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, twilight forest clearing with thatched lodges, a sky full of slow-flying pale moths, lantern lights in the trees, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval high-elf, no figures

hubs.name: Silverleaf Lodge
hubs.description: The Sunward Elen heart-lodge — a long ancestral building grown half into the trunks of three silver birches, windows shuttered with horn-glass, the moot-fire lit by the same spark since the lodge's founding. Lodge-Mother Iselle is older than anyone is willing to estimate aloud.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, ancient Sunward Elen lodge built into three silver birch trunks, glowing horn-glass windows, twilight forest, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval high-elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The Moth-Pillar
landmarks.description: A standing stone the Sunward Elen carved long enough ago that the carvings have weathered into pure pattern. The moths of the wood spend their twilights circling it.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, ancient mossy standing stone in twilight forest, surrounded by slow circling pale moths, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval high-elf fey, no figures

landmarks.name: Silverleaf Falls
landmarks.description: A waterfall where the silver birch litter catches the spray and silvers further. The Sunward Elen don't drink the spray downstream; the deer do.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, woodland waterfall in silver birch forest, silvered leaf-litter in the spray, dappled twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval fey, no figures

landmarks.name: The Hollow-Tree House
landmarks.description: A vast birch hollow that a Sunward Elen scholar made into a single-room library two centuries ago. The scholar is gone. The library, by some accounts, is not.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, hollow inside an enormous silver birch trunk, shelves of weathered scrolls and a single chair, dim warm lantern light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval high-elf, no figures

landmarks.name: The First Grove-Stone
landmarks.description: The earliest carved Sunward Elen stone in the wood — older than the lodge, older than the Concord, possibly older than the fact of the wood itself. Visitors are asked not to touch.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weathered ancient carved standing stone in a clearing of silver birch, moss in the carvings, dappled twilight, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

zones.description: The Sunward Elen heart-wood — silver birch under a lattice of moth-flight, ancestral lodges built into living trunks, the kind of place where outsiders are watched politely for an unreasonable length of time before anyone offers them tea. The Elder Grove ruin at the wood's edge has started speaking again, which the lodge-elders refuse to discuss.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, silver birch forest at twilight, moths on slow wing through pale shafts of light, mossy lodge in the trees, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval high-elf, no figures
zones.vibe: Sunward Elen heart-wood — silver birches, moth-hollows, old courtesy.

zones.name: Skarncamp Wastes
zones.notes: Mid zone in the eastern fjords. Targets 11-30 band session math.

hubs.name: Ravenmeet Longhall
hubs.description: The Skarn mid-capital — a longhall on a basalt wedge where two raid-coastlines meet. Chieftain-Auntie Eira keeps the war-rolls and the marriage-rolls in the same ledger, with annotations that explain why both matter to the same political question.

hubs.name: Salt Watch
hubs.description: The salt-watch waypoint above the prison — a stone watch-house and a single inn, run by a former chieftain named Veska who lost a feud and keeps her dignity by running the watch better than she ran her hold.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Skarn salt-watch tower on a basalt headland above the sea, single inn beside, salt-rust weathering on stone, cold dusk light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking, no figures

hubs.name: War-Stake Camp
hubs.description: A staging camp for the Skarn raid-fleets — earthwork ditch, wooden palisade, a raid-master who gets the drinking finished before sunset and the ships off the stone before dawn. New arrivals learn how to clean salt off iron without scrubbing through to bone.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Skarn raid-staging camp on a basalt shoreline, timber palisade and earthwork, longships at low-tide stocks, dim leaden light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking, no figures

landmarks.name: The Widow's Landing
landmarks.description: A weed-grown stone landing where Skarn widows traditionally wait for ships that never come back. The waiting is formal and respected; the ships are still tracked in the rolls.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, weed-grown stone landing on a basalt shoreline above a cold fjord, salt-rust on the stones, leaden cold afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking, no figures

landmarks.name: The War-Stake Circle
landmarks.description: A ring of driven war-stakes around a turf clearing where raid-captains formally challenge each other. The stakes are replaced as they rot; the names on them aren't.

landmarks.name: The Seabird Cliff
landmarks.description: A cliff used by every coast-bird in the wastes for nesting. The Skarn climb it once a year for eggs. The climbing is ritualised and partly suicidal.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, sheer black basalt sea cliff full of nesting seabirds, salt-spray, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Burnt Longhall
landmarks.description: A burnt-out Skarn longhall from a feud forty years back. The hall was left as a warning. The warning has weathered into a kind of memorial.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, burnt-out Skarn timber longhall on a windswept basalt slope, blackened beams, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking, oppressive mood, no figures

zones.description: The Skarn war-camp belt north of Skarnreach — basalt scrub, raid-staging fields, the salt-watch prison cut into a headland and run by chieftains who lost a feud. The Skarn use it for both: training the young and forgetting the old. New arrivals get put on watchtower rotation; the watchtower rotation is how you find out who you really are.

zones.vibe: Skarn war-camp country — fjord scrub, raid-staging, prison-coast.

zones.name: Skarnreach
zones.notes: Starter zone in the eastern fjords. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Bloodwater Landing
hubs.description: The Skarn's working dock — a stone quay and shipwright's yard at the foot of the Longhall basalt. Three half-built longships at any time, two crews drinking, one fight that is settled by the time the tide comes in. The name is literal; the quay-stone was worked by Skarn who didn't all survive the working.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, working Skarn shipwright quay at the foot of basalt cliffs, half-built longships on stocks, smoke from forge fires, cold afternoon pewter light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking, no figures

hubs.name: Skarn Longhall
hubs.description: The Skarn capital — a great turf-roofed longhall on a basalt headland over the Bloodwater fjord. Chieftain Halvor's mead-bench is the only seat that everyone stands when he stands. He doesn't stand often. The raid-rolls go back further than the Concord oath, and Halvor is content to let outsiders read them, as a kind of warning.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, Skarn timber longhall with turf roof on a basalt headland above a fjord, longships moored below, salt mist, low pewter light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking, no figures

landmarks.name: The Raid-Stone
landmarks.description: A standing stone above the longhall on which every successful raid is carved. The Skarn add lines themselves; the Concord would like a copy and have been politely refused for three generations.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, standing stone on a basalt headland above a fjord, dense carved lines covering every face, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking, no figures

landmarks.name: Widow's Cliff
landmarks.description: A cliff edge where Skarn widows traditionally throw the broken weapons of dead warriors into the sea. Locals know which corner the tide returns the iron to.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, sheer basalt cliff above a cold fjord, rusted weapons in cracks of the rock at the edge, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Seal Rocks
landmarks.description: A flat shelf of rocks at the foot of the fjord where seals haul out every spring. The Skarn hunt them by formal agreement, and the seals seem to accept the arrangement.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, flat sea-rocks at the foot of a fjord, seals hauled out, salt-spray, cold afternoon pewter light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Burial-Ship Mound
landmarks.description: A turfed mound where the Skarn have buried at least one longship, possibly three. The Skarn insist the count is personal. The mound is taller than any nearby hill.

zones.description: The Skarn starter, hard against the eastern fjords. The Longhall sits on a basalt headland over Bloodwater Landing; the Skarn measure status by what you've raided and how honestly you talk about it. Pact-allied by treaty, but the old chieftains still sail past Concord cliffs at night and come back with stories nobody wrote down.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, narrow cold fjord, basalt headland with timber longhall, longships moored below, low pewter sky, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval viking aesthetic, no figures
zones.vibe: Skarn fjord-home — longhalls, skin-boats, salt and iron.

zones.name: Stoneguard Deep
zones.notes: Starter zone in the iron mountains. Targets 1-10 band session math.

hubs.name: Anvilgate
hubs.description: Forward outpost at the rim of the iron mountains, where the hall-roads meet daylight. Stone gatehouse, shift-bell, a handful of pickaxe veterans who came up from the lower halls and don't intend to go back. Captain Drun has been short two patrols since the rockworms moved up; new sworn bring their own rope.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, mountain-rim Hearthkin outpost at the mouth of an under-hall, stone gatehouse, banked forge braziers, snow on the upper slopes, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dwarven, no figures

hubs.name: Stoneguard Halls
hubs.description: The Hearthkin under-capital — a great vaulted hall-city carved into living granite, lit by suspended forge-lamps and ventilated by shafts that whistle in winter. Anvil-Mother Brytta keeps the rolls and the temper of the place in equal measure. The lower halls are sealed pending a rockworm count that nobody wants to actually conduct.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, vast Hearthkin underground hall carved into granite, suspended forge-lamps, basalt columns, busy anvil-yards, warm forge glow, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dwarven, no figures

landmarks.name: Forge-Chimney Ridge
landmarks.description: A ridge venting permanent forge-smoke from the deeper halls below. The Hearthkin keep the chimneys clear by rota; the rota has been short-handed since the rockworms moved up.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, mountain ridge venting steady forge smoke from carved stone chimneys, snow on the slopes, leaden afternoon light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dwarven, no figures

landmarks.name: The Collapsed Lower Stair
landmarks.description: A grand stair down to the abandoned lower halls, blocked at the third landing by a controlled collapse. The Hearthkin sealed it after the rockworms; somebody has reopened the seal twice.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, vast carved stone stair descending into darkness, broken at a landing by a deliberate rockfall, dim warm lamp glow, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dwarven, no figures

landmarks.name: The Granite Pillar Grove
landmarks.description: A natural cavern outside the city's gates where forty-three basalt pillars stand in a rough circle. The Hearthkin say the pillars predate them; the carvings on them say otherwise.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, cathedral-sized natural cavern with forty tall basalt pillars in a rough circle, lantern light at the base, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Rockworm Breach
landmarks.description: A jagged tunnel mouth where the rockworms came up last winter. Sealed twice, breached three times. The Hearthkin patrol the edge of it on rotation; they don't go inside.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, jagged tunnel breach in a mountainside, scattered Hearthkin pickaxes and broken seal-stones, dim warm light from inside, painterly atmospheric, no figures

zones.description: The Hearthkin starter under the western shoulder of the Iron Mountains. Lantern-lit hall-roads, bell-shifts in the deep forges, hot iron and cold stone in equal measure. The rockworms moved up from the abandoned levels last winter and Anvilgate is short-handed; new recruits get rope-and-pick the morning they swear in. Concord by treaty since the Third Concord; Hearthkin in temperament forever.
zones.prompt: wide establishing landscape, mountain-skirt fortress town carved into a granite shoulder, banded smokestacks, lantern-lit switchbacks, late-afternoon overcast cool light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures, no characters
zones.vibe: Hearthkin under-halls — orderly, bell-disciplined, smoke and stone.

zones.name: The Sundering Mines
zones.notes: Endgame zone in the ruin line. Targets 46-60 band session math.

hubs.name: The Lightless Gallery
hubs.description: The deep gallery proper — a vast unlit hall that was mined to its current shape and then abandoned. The Hearthkin say the original miners chose to abandon it. The records say their captains chose for them. The deeper lights are not provided by either.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, vast unlit deep mining gallery, abandoned scaffolds and ore wagons, faint distant phosphorescence, oppressive dark mood, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Mineshaft Alpha
hubs.description: A deeper waypoint inside the upper galleries — a single stone bunkhouse cut into the wall, a watch-station, a list of provisions that everyone signs in and signs out of. The list is not always complete in the same direction.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, deep mine gallery waypoint, stone bunkhouse cut into the wall, lantern-lit corridor, scaffold timbers, dim warm-cold light, painterly atmospheric, no figures

hubs.name: Old-Tunnel Camp
hubs.description: A surface camp at the mouth of the upper sundering tunnels — Hearthkin scaffolds, two veteran patrol leaders, and a single stone-cut chapel for the miners who have stopped coming back. The chapel is still consecrated, more or less, and nobody is sure who is performing the upkeep.
hubs.prompt: establishing shot, surface mining camp at the mouth of abandoned mountain tunnels, scaffolded shafts and stone chapel, leaden afternoon cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval, no figures

landmarks.name: The Dark Chimney
landmarks.description: A vertical mine-shaft so deep that the Hearthkin who surveyed it never found the bottom. Outsiders are welcome to lower ropes; the ropes do not return alone.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, vast vertical mine-shaft entrance carved into a mountain wall, dark below, abandoned scaffolds at the rim, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Scaffold Grave
landmarks.description: A cluster of pickaxe-handles driven into the rock as a memorial to Hearthkin lost in a single bad shift. There are sixty-two handles. The dead were forty-seven.
landmarks.prompt: establishing shot, cluster of weathered Hearthkin pickaxe handles driven into a mountain wall as memorials, leaden cold light, painterly atmospheric, late-medieval dwarven, oppressive mood, no figures

landmarks.name: The Phosphor Pool
landmarks.description: A subterranean pool the Skrel scavengers have started harvesting for phosphor-fungus. The Hearthkin disapprove; the phosphor doesn't know the difference.

dungeons.description:
The oldest thing on the Spine and older than both flags below it.
The Elder Wyrm has slept since the second Concord and the snow
above her lair has not melted in any of those years; the
Hearthkin scout-charts mark the spot with a single careful glyph
that means 'do not go here.' Crown of Ruin business has woken her
— she felt the seal break at Sundered Peak, and she felt the
contested title go unsettled, and she will not sleep through
that. Twenty-man raid. The longest fight in the world.

is another shrine, and beneath that, a fire. The First Flame was
burning before either faction had a name to be loyal to, and the
failed prayer of the Twin Saints still echoes off its walls in two
languages that don't recognize each other. Pilgrims from both
faiths come this far down and turn back. The few who go further do
not return as themselves. Ten-man raid country. Bring water.

has yet been paid out.

Below the Lightless Gallery the mines stop being mines. Carver-
Mother's kin breed in the lower vents and the air smells of the
metal that comes off them — sour, bright, wrong. Hearthkin scouts
map this far and then turn back without writing it down. The
Deeper Thing is the reason the Coming reached as far as it did,
though no historian alive will put that sentence in ink. Ten-man
raid; the longest pull in the game ends here.

bosses.description:
The yeti the others bring meat to. He sits in the deep gallery
ringed by frost-bound ancestor poles, and when the fight goes
against him he calls his dead pack back as cold-shrouded shades —
claws still working, hunger still on them. There is a phase, brief
and bad, where ordinary cold cannot touch him. The shaman knew the
Spine before the Coming did.

dungeons.description:
A network of ice-mammal galleries gnawed beneath the Spine's western
shoulder. The trapper Hesska keeps a tally-stick at her hut on the
surface — one notch for every party that doesn't come back, and she
has stopped pretending the count is for anyone but herself. The
warren stinks of wet fur and old blood. Five-man country. The cold
here is the kind that kills the careless before any beast does.
dungeons.prompt: establishing shot, dungeon interior, vast ice cavern strewn with bone piles and frozen carcasses, blue glacial light, mammoth tusks embedded in the walls, painterly atmospheric, oppressive mood, no figures
